title: Farï¿½farï¿½away
content:
What sight so lured him throï¿½ the fields he knew
As where earthï¿½s green stole into heavenï¿½s own hue,

Farï¿½farï¿½away?

What sound was dearest in his native dells?
The mellow lin-lan-lone of evening bells

Farï¿½farï¿½away.

What vague world-whisper, mystic pain or joy,
Throï¿½ those three words would haunt him when a boy,

Farï¿½farï¿½away?

A whisper from his dawn of life? a breath
From some fair dawn beyond the doors of death

Farï¿½farï¿½away?

Far, far, how far? from oï¿½er the gates of Birth,
The faint horizons, all the bounds of earth,

Farï¿½farï¿½away?

What charm in words, a charm no words could give?
O dying words, can Music make you live

Farï¿½farï¿½away?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alfred Lord Tennyson delivers a powerful performance in "Farï¿½farï¿½away"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
